Title: Photosystem Perturbation by Mutations Staygreen Confers Allele-Dependent Defences Against Infections of Pathogens With Different Lifestyles and Abiotic Stress Tolerance

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Staygreen (SGR) magnesium dechelatase; chloroplast transit peptide; conserved SGR domain; variable C-terminal region
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthesis; maintenance of green tissues; stress-associated chlorophyll homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function or weak-loss-of-function alleles reduce chlorophyll degradation, delay senescence/stay-green, enhance multiple disease resistance and chilling tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsSGR is the causal gene underlying the cucumber staygreen/MDR locus, with a natural A323G (Q108R) allele and a CRISPR-derived 37-bp deletion (SGRΔ37) both conferring enhanced resistance to multiple pathogens and improved low-temperature tolerance. 2. Mutant alleles reduce pathogen-induced chlorophyll degradation, maintain higher chlorophyll content and PSII efficiency, and delay senescence, providing passive defence. 3. Transcriptome analyses support additional active defence via SA/JA-associated pathways likely linked to ROS-mediated retrograde signalling. 4. SGRΔ37 is a stronger allele than A323G/Q108R for stay-green, MDR, and chilling tolerance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Near-isogenic lines; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; disease phenotyping against multiple pathogens; chlorophyll quantification; Fv/Fm measurement; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; transient overexpression in tobacco; subcellular localization; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; co-IP; protein structure prediction
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CsSGR; CsLHCB1.1; CsLHCB1.2; CsLHCB1.3; CsLHCB1.4; CsLHCB1.5; CsPPH; CsPAO; CsRCCR; CsNOL; CsNYC1; CsHCAR; CsBCM
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced upon pathogen infection; expression higher in susceptible WT/NIL-S than resistant mutant lines during infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Pcu, Cor, and Psl infection; reduced in Cssgr mutant backgrounds relative to susceptible controls
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for transient overexpression/localization assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infection with Pseudoperonospora cubensis, Corynespora cassiicola, Pseudomonas syringae pv. lachrymans, powdery mildew, Alternaria leaf spot; low-temperature chilling at 13/10°C day/night
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flowering time delayed by 2-3 days in KO lines; no evident penalty for plant architecture, fruit quality, or yield-related traits in greenhouse tests
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for gene editing to generate strong stay-green alleles for multiple disease resistance and chilling tolerance with minimal horticultural penalties
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SGR is a chlorophyll catabolic enzyme component, the paper emphasizes it as a regulatory hub in chlorophyll degradation/photosystem interactions rather than extracting it as a pathway enzyme. The natural mutant is described as A323G causing Q108R substitution; CRISPR SGRΔ37 is a stronger loss-of-function allele.
